mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income drives consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates across a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets, which reduces wage-push inflation fears and allows the Fed to maintain accommodative policy, supporting equity multiples.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 5.5% in more than 40% of observations over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity valuations across cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment, forward guidance embedded in the federal funds rate path anchors long-term yields, compressing the equity risk premium and driving index appreciation.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly higher than the historical median when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, or the signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect capacity utilization improvements and supply chain normalization, which expand profit margins and drive positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly SPX returns show no positive relationship with INDPRO month-over-month changes, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing permits signal future construction activity and consumer confidence, which historically lead equity market rallies as investors price in stronger domestic demand.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when HOUST exceeds the 3-month growth threshold in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth combined with stable inflation expectations reduces the probability of premature monetary tightening, allowing equity valuations to expand without a discount rate shock.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/CPIAUCSL signal fails to generate positive 20-day SPX returns in over 50% of observations across a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the trend rate signals robust corporate revenue expansion, which directly translates into higher earnings per share and positive equity index performance over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not outperform the baseline median when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the forward return distribution shows no skew toward positive values in triggered periods.
